watchFor: AVOID: Entering short puts without a pre-defined stop order — 'no stop was set' appeared explicitly in WMT reasoning, meaning risk was undefined at entry

AVOID: 38% win rate over 29 cumulative trades with heavy loss days (-$4,757 on Feb 11, -$2,870 on Feb 10, -$2,157 on Feb 13) signals a position sizing or strategy-fit problem that hasn't been resolved
AVOID: Monitoring frequency increased on INTC during stress (4 checks in 20 minutes) — a sign of emotional engagement that can lead to premature exits
tomorrowRules: Before entering any short put: set a GTC stop-limit order immediately at entry. Do not enter the position without a stop in the system. No exceptions.
With VIX above 28, reduce short put size by 50% or require a higher premium threshold to justify the elevated gamma risk.
If you check a position more than twice in 20 minutes, step away for 15 minutes before making any decision — frequent monitoring in volatile markets leads to noise-driven exits.

dailyLessons.gradingReason: The agent demonstrated disciplined stop management on INTC and correctly closed WMT before losses accelerated. No day trades executed, which is appropriate given VIX at 31+ and choppy RISK_OFF conditions. The swing account P&L is flat today, suggesting prudent inactivity. Grade capped at B due to the systemic issue of entering short puts with no stop pre-set (visible in WMT close reasoning), which is a recurring risk management gap.
keyLessons: Stop-loss must be set at entry, not reactively after a position moves against you. WMT was closed at +14.18% loss because no stop was pre-set — this is the second time this pattern appears in the logs. Build stop into the order entry workflow, not as an afterthought.
The agent correctly identified VIX 31+ and Iran war headlines as reasons to reduce exposure. When macro risk is elevated, reduce position size or sit out entirely — not just monitor more frequently.
INTC short put recovered and became profitable (+5.56%) partly due to disciplined hold with a defined stop at $1.25. This is the correct process — define the stop, then let the thesis play out.

goodPatterns: Correctly closed WMT position when momentum turned against the short put in a risk-off environment rather than hoping for reversal
Set and referenced a specific stop level ($1.25) on INTC and did not panic-close during minor adverse moves (-2.78%)
Stayed out of day trading entirely during elevated VIX and choppy conditions — capital preservation over forced activity

Monitoring frequency increased on INTC during stress (4 checks in 20 minutes) — a sign of emotional engagement that can lead to premature exits

tomorrowRules: Before entering any short put: set a GTC stop-limit order immediately at entry. Do not enter the position without a stop in the system. No exceptions.
With VIX above 28, reduce short put size by 50% or require a higher premium threshold to justify the elevated gamma risk.
If you check a position more than twice in 20 minutes, step away for 15 minutes before making any decision — frequent monitoring in volatile markets leads to noise-driven exits.
dailyLessons.riskAssessment: Partially adequate. INTC risk was managed with a defined stop and disciplined holds. WMT exposed the core weakness: undefined risk at entry. In a VIX 31 environment with geopolitical headlines, selling puts without pre-set stops is playing with an open position in a minefield. The agent recovered by closing WMT reactively, but the damage limitation was luck of timing, not process. The cumulative stats show $9,626 in losses over just four bad days (Feb 10-13) — position sizing relative to account equity needs review.
dailyLessons.emotionalCheck: No signs of revenge trading today — no day trades were forced despite the availability of the $90K day trade account. The repeated monitoring of INTC (4 rapid-fire checks) suggests anxiety around the position but discipline held. The decision to sit out day trading entirely in a choppy VIX 31 market is a mature call. Overall emotional discipline today was above average compared to the Feb 10-13 stretch where multiple losing days compounded into large drawdowns.
